SimpleCoder: 
import re
import argparse

def regex_tester():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="CLI Regex Tester")
    parser.add_argument("regex", type=str, help="Regular expression to test")
    parser.add_argument("test_string", type=str, help="String to test against the regex")
    parser.add_argument("--show-groups", action="store_true", help="Option to show matched groups")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    pattern = re.compile(args.regex)
    match = pattern.match(args.test_string)

    if match:
        print("The string matches the provided regex.")
        if args.show_groups:
            print("Matched groups:", match.groups())
    else:
        print("The string does not match the provided regex.")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    regex_tester()

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[2025-05-13 08:51:36] NEW MESSAGES:

SimpleTester: 
import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch
from io import StringIO
import sys

# Assuming the function is saved in a file named regex_tester.py
from regex_tester import regex_tester

@pytest.mark.parametrize("regex,test_string,expected_output", [
    (r"\d+", "12345", "The string matches the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"\d+", "abcde", "The string does not match the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"abc", "abcdef", "The string matches the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"^abc$", "abcdef", "The string does not match the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"(abc)", "abcdef", "The string matches the provided regex.\nMatched groups: ('abc',)\n"),
])
def test_regex_tester(regex, test_string, expected_output, monkeypatch):
    args = ["prog", regex, test_string]
    if "Matched groups" in expected_output:
        args.append("--show-groups")
    monkeypatch.setattr(sys, "argv", args)

    with patch('sys.stdout', new=StringIO()) as fake_out:
        regex_tester()
        assert fake_out.getvalue() == expected_output

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.mark.parametrize("regex,test_string,expected_output", [
    (r"\d+\.\d*", "123.45", "The string matches the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"a.*c", "abc", "The string matches the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"^$", "", "The string matches the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"foo|bar", "foobar", "The string matches the provided regex.\n"),
    (r"\w+\s", "hello ", "The string matches the provided regex.\nMatched groups: ('hello',)\n"),
])
def test_regex_tester_advanced(regex, test_string, expected_output, monkeypatch):
    args = ["prog", regex, test_string]
    if "Matched groups" in expected_output:
        args.append("--show-groups")
    monkeypatch.setattr(sys, "argv", args)

    with patch('sys.stdout', new=StringIO()) as fake_out:
        regex_tester()
        assert fake_out.getvalue() == expected_output
